name: Yext v/s SOCi v/s Synup
section_which_is_best_for_you:
Yext v/s SOCi v/s Synup: Which one should you choose?

If you’re a marketer working for an agency or a SMB, you’re always on the lookout for the best kind of tools. Even more so, if you’re managing Local SEO for your clients and are confused about the tools you should pick and choose from. 

In this article, we shall compare Yext v/s SOCi v/s Synup. 

All of these tools are one in a kind, but it often comes down to why you should pick one amongst the many. 

We'll explore the features, pros, and cons of each product to understand their strengths and weaknesses. 

With this article, we hope that you’re able to pick and choose the right kind of tool for your requirements - be it an SMB, Startup, agency, Enterprise, or a consultant managing multiple Local SEO clients!

Yext v/s SOCi v/s Synup: At a glance!

Often a preferred choice amongst marketers, Yext, provides you with a comprehensive toolset of a kind where it covers listings, reviews, social media management, store locator pages, and much more. It's user-friendly with great integrations, but it can be pricey for more advanced features. 

SOCi focuses on social media and reputation management, offering tools that help businesses manage multiple social media accounts and online reviews from a single platform. It provides analytics and reporting features and is ideal for multi-location businesses looking to efficiently manage and optimize their social media presence. However, users feel that it lacks certain capabilities and has limited third-party integrations. 

Synup offers an all-in-one solution for managing local business online presence effectively and affordably. It simplifies Local SEO management while also catering to social media needs, all at a nominal cost. With Synup, businesses can ensure accurate listings across various online directories, monitor reviews from platforms like Google and Yelp, and gain valuable insights to enhance local SEO performance and competitive edge. The platform assists in managing citations, resolving duplicate listings, and optimizing Google My Business profiles, thereby boosting local visibility and fostering better customer engagement. It is suitable for businesses of all sizes and is extremely agency-focused. 

‍

‍
section_listing_management:
Yext v/s SOCi v/s Synup: Pricing 
‍

Source: Review hosted and collected by G2

 

Synup is priced affordably compared to Yext and SOCi, fulfilling most of their functionalities. Many users find Synup's services to be great value for money, offering comprehensive solutions and a helpful team always ready to assist with any questions or concerns.

Yext v/s SOCi v/s Synup: Listings Management 

When it comes to listings management, Yext has a wide publisher network of 200+ directories and search engines, however, one must keep in mind that, if migrating to a different tool people might end up losing mostly 50 or 60% of their data which has to be re-done from scratch. 

SOCi has a great listing management system, however, it does lack bulk verification support when it comes to listings management, which might make it a lot more inconvenient for users to add multiple listings together. 

When it comes to Synup, it offers a complete listings management system that is fully API-based, capable of handling bulk verification and it does not own your listings either, so when you’re migrating to a different platform, you won’t lose out on your data. 

Yext v/s SOCi v/s Synup: Reputation Management 

Yext has an all-in-one reputation management system, which comes with various features such as Generative Review Response, First-party review generation feedback, and a few more, however, users as skeptical about signing up for Yext as it charges exorbitant fees, and their customer support is too big to care. 

When it comes to SOCi, their reputation management system is pretty versatile, SOCi Genius AI helps with prompt review responses, and review generation and comes with fully customizable widgets. However, users mention that they are priced too high and might not be suitable for everyone. 

Synup comes with a review management system that is fully customizable and AI-enabled. It helps with generating new responses, comes with customizable templates and review responses that can be used, and comes with a smart response system that helps respond to reviews easily. It is also priced fairly for agencies, resellers, and SMBs, or anyone for that matter, looking to streamline their reputation management. 

Yext v/s SOCi v/s Synup: Local SEO 

Yext offers features such as Rank Tracker, Store Locator Pages, AI-powered chatbot for landing pages, and more that are heavily focused on enterprises and large-scale businesses. 

SOCi supports store locator pages and locators that help businesses amplify their local search visibility, however, customers say that these are limited features compared to other competitors. 

‍

Synup offers end-to-end Local SEO features for businesses of all sizes. These include keyword rank tracker, grid rank tracker, store locator pages, store locator, voice-search optimizer, and even a social media platform. One unique feature that sets Synup apart is hyper-localized campaigns, these campaigns allow you to run promotions and offers triggered by specific times and events, such as weather changes or festive occasions.

Yext v/s SOCi v/s Synup: Managed Services 

Compared to Yext and SOCi, Synup offers an extensive range of managed services, including listings, reviews, social, store locator pages, and more. With Synup, you can also white-label its managed services to better support your clients without having to handle everything yourself. 

While Yext does provide expert services, their high charges often deter potential users. On the other hand, SOCi does not provide Managed Services of any sort to its customers. 

Yext v/s SOCi v/s Synup: Reseller Support 

When it comes to reseller support, Synup comes first! Since it offers end-to-end tools and support for resellers it allows not only white-labeling its resources, but also helps manage your client's listings, and reputation, and find new leads and prospects better, thus ensuring that the agency is running smoothly. It also offers a white-label business scan tool that you can use to capture your leads better and integrate them into your website.

SOCi is somewhat reseller-focused by providing them with a partner API that helps them create Facebook local pages, boost multiple posts, and respond to reviews from the same dashboard - they also help you white label their social and review management platform and pass it on to your clients. These, although, very basic features in comparison to Synup is what SOCi offers to its resellers. 

Yext provides integration and partnership programs for agencies, consultants, and other SaaS resellers/platforms. However, the support you receive after partnering is limited. Since Yext focuses more on the enterprise segment, they invest less in helping their partners grow their businesses.

Yext v/s SOCi v/s Synup: Which Should You Choose? 

While Yext and SOCi have some great features, a few important differences are still prominent. Users in this day and age, are looking for reliability and someone they can trust. While SOCi might fit the bill for most of it, it has limited capabilities when compared to Synup when it comes to Local SEO. Yext on the other hand, needs to improve on their after-sales services and prices extensively. 
‍

Final Words

In conclusion, if you’re seeking an alternative that excels in third-party integrations, after-sales support, and affordability, Synup is the perfect choice! It offers of managed services and white-labeling features, making it the perfect choice for resellers and agencies. Moreover, Synup ensures that your data remains secure during any migration process, eliminating concerns about data loss when switching services. When comparing Synup to Yext and SOCi, it stands out as the clear winner. 

Yext v/s SOCi v/s Synup: FAQs

How much does Synup cost? 

When looking at how much Synup costs, it is often less as compared to its competitors. Their standard plan starts at $30 Per Month. They also offer customizable plans to different requirements if you reach out to them and enquire about it. 

What does Yext do? 

Although, Yext is one of the largest players, what does Yext do is a frequently asked question. To answer the question, Yext provides a comprehensive platform for managing digital knowledge, including business listings, reviews, and local SEO. It helps businesses ensure accurate and consistent information across various online directories, search engines, and social media platforms. Additionally, Yext offers tools for review management, analytics, and page building, all aimed at improving online presence and search visibility.

Is Yext a big company? 

Yext is a big company. It is a leading provider of digital knowledge management and local SEO, serving a wide range of clients from small businesses to large enterprises.
